iterations.title: Map Claude plugin structure; audit current layout against it
Resolve the packaging forks (F1-F7) and slice execution
E1: move engine to repo root + plugin skeleton

verdicts.reason: Use a src/ layout: package at ./src/lume, not flat ./lume at root (avoids the repo/package name echo and keeps root uncluttered; conventional Python src-layout).

iterations.title: E2: one guiding skill that drives the lume CLI loop
E3: bootstrap-on-seed + front-door README + marketplace + dogfooding + smoke test

plan.sketch: E1: move engine to repo root (./lume package, ./bin/lume, tests/) + add .claude-plugin/plugin.json; bin/lume resolves the package via its own location; .lume/ stays in-repo state only and is excluded from plugin content. DoD: lume runs as a bare command from the new entry point; 227 tests green at new paths; our own dogfooding loop still works.

E2: skill/command wrappers - namespaced SKILL.md per loop step (seed, status, open, approve/start, handback, accept) that call bundled lume by bare name and relay --json. DoD: each loop step invocable as a plugin skill; commands map onto existing verbs with no logic duplicated.

E3: root README (what/why/install/iteration-loop/first-command, command list derived from verb catalog) + documented install path (marketplace add + /plugin install; --plugin-dir for dev) + clean-repo install smoke test proving 'lume seed' then a first iteration works on a fresh repo that is not this one. DoD: a stranger can follow the README to their first iteration; smoke test passes.

Closeout: retro (did packaging buy back its ceremony?), assumptions checked vs evidence (esp. .lume-not-shipped mechanism + behaviour-preservation), objective done-when assessed, decisions logged, lume close.

workstream.title: Package lume as an installable Claude plugin
